pick.rationale: Arkansas enters as a legitimate top-20 team ranked 17th in KenPom and 20th in Torvik with an elite offense ranking 5th nationally in adjusted offensive efficiency, while Hawai'i sits at 105th in SRS—a gap of roughly 85 spots that reflects the massive difference between SEC and Big West competition. The Razorbacks just won the SEC tournament championship behind Darius Acuff Jr.'s 30-point performance in the final, proving their ability to perform under pressure against elite competition. Hawai'i's 8th-ranked defense is impressive and could keep the game competitive for stretches, but their 177th-ranked offense will struggle mightily against Arkansas's 50th-ranked defense, and the Rainbow Warriors haven't faced a single opponent close to Arkansas's caliber all season with a schedule strength ranked 227th. The talent gap, John Calipari's tournament experience, and Arkansas's momentum from their SEC title run make this a straightforward 4/13 matchup with minimal upset risk.
